titleZh: 我们到底花了多少时间在手机上？
titleEn: How much time are we actually on our phones?
summaryEn: An HSK 5 reading titled “How much time are we actually on our phones?”
textZh:
讨论《我们到底花了多少时间在手机上？》时，最容易犯的错误，是只看到表面的现象。
一个现象能够长期存在，通常不是因为某一个人突然做了决定。
它往往和市场、制度、技术、家庭选择以及社会心理同时有关。
因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
第一，是需求的变化。
年轻人、家庭和城市居民的生活方式正在改变。
他们更重视效率，也更重视体验。
如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
第二，是供给的变化。
企业为了获得用户，会不断降低门槛、改进流程、推出新的功能。
平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
但是，选择越多，人们有时候反而越焦虑。
第三，是社会评价的变化。
很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。
他们也会考虑这种选择是否显得体面、是否符合朋友的期待、是否适合放在社交媒体上。
这说明消费和身份之间的关系越来越紧密。
从这个角度看，人们花在手机上的时间不是孤立的现象。
它是现代城市生活的一面镜子。
当然，我们不能只把变化理解成进步。
新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。
比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。
信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。
服务更方便以后，个人数据和隐私也需要更多保护。
所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
这个问题没有一个简单答案。
但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
它会进入更深的社会理解。
进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
translation: When discussing 'How much time are we actually on our phones?', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon. When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision. It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology. Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together. The first layer is changing demand. The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing. They care more about efficiency and also more about experience. If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept. The second layer is changing supply. To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions. Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster. However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become. The third layer is changing social evaluation. Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons. They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media. This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected. From this perspective, How much time are we actually on our phones?？ is not an isolated event. It is a mirror of modern urban life. Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress. When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear. For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed. After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see. After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection. Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap. We must also ask to whom it transfers costs. Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for? This question has no simple answer. But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly. I believe a mature society should not reject new things. At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better. A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing. In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life. Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure. This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion. In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life. Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency. This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep. It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves. However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources. After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for. Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools. We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools. If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized. If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed. If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified. These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life. For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure. When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated. When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it. Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling. It will enter a deeper social understanding. Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time.

sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: From this perspective, the time people spend on their phones is not an isolated phenomenon.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它是现代城市生活的一面镜子。
sentenceExplanations.en: It is a mirror of modern urban life.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It is a mirror of modern urban life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 当然，我们不能只把变化理解成进步。
sentenceExplanations.en: Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。
sentenceExplanations.en: When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。
sentenceExplanations.en: For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。
sentenceExplanations.en: After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 服务更方便以后，个人数据和隐私也需要更多保护。
sentenceExplanations.en: After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
sentenceExplanations.en: Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
sentenceExplanations.en: We must also ask to whom it transfers costs.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We must also ask to whom it transfers costs.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
grammar.exampleEn: We must also ask to whom it transfers costs.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
sentenceExplanations.en: Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for?
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for?

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这个问题没有一个简单答案。
sentenceExplanations.en: This question has no simple answer.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This question has no simple answer.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
sentenceExplanations.en: But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
sentenceExplanations.en: I believe a mature society should not reject new things.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I believe a mature society should not reject new things.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
sentenceExplanations.en: At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
sentenceExplanations.en: A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
sentenceExplanations.en: In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life.

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
grammar.exampleEn: In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
sentenceExplanations.en: Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
sentenceExplanations.en: This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
sentenceExplanations.en: In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
sentenceExplanations.en: Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.

grammar.label: Gradual change
grammar.explanationEn: This form is followed by a quality or state to show gradual change over time: “more and more …”.
grammar.exampleZh: 现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
grammar.exampleEn: Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
sentenceExplanations.en: This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
sentenceExplanations.en: It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
sentenceExplanations.en: However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
sentenceExplanations.en: After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
sentenceExplanations.en: Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
sentenceExplanations.en: We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
sentenceExplanations.en: If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
grammar.exampleEn: If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
sentenceExplanations.en: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
grammar.exampleEn: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
sentenceExplanations.en: If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
grammar.exampleEn: If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
sentenceExplanations.en: These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
sentenceExplanations.en: For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
sentenceExplanations.en: When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
sentenceExplanations.en: When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
sentenceExplanations.en: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.

grammar.label: Necessary condition
grammar.explanationEn: The opening phrase sets a necessary condition, and the second marker introduces the result that can happen only under that condition.
grammar.exampleZh: 只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
grammar.exampleEn: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
grammar.exampleEn: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它会进入更深的社会理解。
sentenceExplanations.en: It will enter a deeper social understanding.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It will enter a deeper social understanding.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
sentenceExplanations.en: Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the main topic of this reading?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 我们到底花了多少时间在手机上？
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: It mainly discusses how much time are we actually on our phones?.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The title and repeated details point to the central topic.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the writer's final thought?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The final sentence usually gives the conclusion or reflection.

titleZh: 中国电动车的快速发展
titleEn: China's rapid rise in electric vehicles
summaryEn: An HSK 5 reading titled “China's rapid rise in electric vehicles”.
textZh:
讨论《中国电动车的快速发展》时，最容易犯的错误，是只看到表面的现象。
一个现象能够长期存在，通常不是因为某一个人突然做了决定。
它往往和市场、制度、技术、家庭选择以及社会心理同时有关。
因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
第一，是需求的变化。
年轻人、家庭和城市居民的生活方式正在改变。
他们更重视效率，也更重视体验。
如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
第二，是供给的变化。
企业为了获得用户，会不断降低门槛、改进流程、推出新的功能。
平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
但是，选择越多，人们有时候反而越焦虑。
第三，是社会评价的变化。
很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。
他们也会考虑这种选择是否显得体面、是否符合朋友的期待、是否适合放在社交媒体上。
这说明消费和身份之间的关系越来越紧密。
从这个角度看，中国电动车的快速发展不是孤立的事件。
它是现代城市生活的一面镜子。
当然，我们不能只把变化理解成进步。
新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。
比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。
信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。
服务更方便以后，个人数据和隐私也需要更多保护。
所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
这个问题没有一个简单答案。
但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
它会进入更深的社会理解。
进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
translation: When discussing 'China's rapid rise in electric vehicles', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon. When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision. It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology. Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together. The first layer is changing demand. The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing. They care more about efficiency and also more about experience. If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept. The second layer is changing supply. To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions. Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster. However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become. The third layer is changing social evaluation. Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons. They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media. This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected. From this perspective, China's rapid rise in electric vehicles is not an isolated event. It is a mirror of modern urban life. Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress. When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear. For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed. After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see. After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection. Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap. We must also ask to whom it transfers costs. Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for? This question has no simple answer. But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly. I believe a mature society should not reject new things. At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better. A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing. In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life. Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure. This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion. Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency. This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep. It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves. However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources. After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for. Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools. We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools. If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized. If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed. If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified. These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life. For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure. When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated. When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it. Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling. It will enter a deeper social understanding. Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time. In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life.

The first layer is changing demand. The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing. They care more about efficiency and also more about experience. If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept. The second layer is changing supply. To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions. Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster. However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become. The third layer is changing social evaluation. Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons. They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media. This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected. From this perspective, China's rapid rise in electric vehicles is not an isolated event. It is a mirror of modern urban life. Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress. When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear. For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed. After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see. After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection. Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap. We must also ask to whom it transfers costs. Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for? This question has no simple answer. But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly. I believe a mature society should not reject new things. At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better. A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing. In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life. Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure. This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion. Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency. This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep. It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves. However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources. After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for. Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools. We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools. If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized. If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed. If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified. These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life. For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure. When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated. When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it. Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling. It will enter a deeper social understanding. Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time. In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life.

paragraphTranslations.en: When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。
paragraphTranslations.en: For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。
paragraphTranslations.en: After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 服务更方便以后，个人数据和隐私也需要更多保护。
paragraphTranslations.en: After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
paragraphTranslations.en: Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
paragraphTranslations.en: We must also ask to whom it transfers costs.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
paragraphTranslations.en: Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for?

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这个问题没有一个简单答案。
paragraphTranslations.en: This question has no simple answer.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
paragraphTranslations.en: But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
paragraphTranslations.en: I believe a mature society should not reject new things.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
paragraphTranslations.en: At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
paragraphTranslations.en: A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
paragraphTranslations.en: In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
paragraphTranslations.en: Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
paragraphTranslations.en: This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
paragraphTranslations.en: Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
paragraphTranslations.en: This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
paragraphTranslations.en: It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
paragraphTranslations.en: However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
paragraphTranslations.en: After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
paragraphTranslations.en: Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
paragraphTranslations.en: We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
paragraphTranslations.en: If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
paragraphTranslations.en: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
paragraphTranslations.en: If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
paragraphTranslations.en: These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
paragraphTranslations.en: For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
paragraphTranslations.en: When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
paragraphTranslations.en: When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
paragraphTranslations.en: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它会进入更深的社会理解。
paragraphTranslations.en: It will enter a deeper social understanding.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
paragraphTranslations.en: Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
paragraphTranslations.en: In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 讨论《中国电动车的快速发展》时，最容易犯的错误，是只看到表面的现象。
sentenceExplanations.en: When discussing 'China's rapid rise in electric vehicles', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When discussing 'China's rapid rise in electric vehicles', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 一个现象能够长期存在，通常不是因为某一个人突然做了决定。
sentenceExplanations.en: When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它往往和市场、制度、技术、家庭选择以及社会心理同时有关。
sentenceExplanations.en: It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
sentenceExplanations.en: Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
grammar.exampleEn: Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第一，是需求的变化。
sentenceExplanations.en: The first layer is changing demand.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The first layer is changing demand.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 年轻人、家庭和城市居民的生活方式正在改变。
sentenceExplanations.en: The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 他们更重视效率，也更重视体验。
sentenceExplanations.en: They care more about efficiency and also more about experience.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: They care more about efficiency and also more about experience.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
sentenceExplanations.en: If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
grammar.exampleEn: If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第二，是供给的变化。
sentenceExplanations.en: The second layer is changing supply.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The second layer is changing supply.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 企业为了获得用户，会不断降低门槛、改进流程、推出新的功能。
sentenceExplanations.en: To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
sentenceExplanations.en: Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object

sentenceExplanations.en: However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第三，是社会评价的变化。
sentenceExplanations.en: The third layer is changing social evaluation.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The third layer is changing social evaluation.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。

sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons.

grammar.label: Only then / not until

sentenceExplanations.zh: 他们也会考虑这种选择是否显得体面、是否符合朋友的期待、是否适合放在社交媒体上。
sentenceExplanations.en: They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media.

sentenceExplanations.en: This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected.

titleZh: 离开家以后才懂的事
titleEn: Things you only understand after leaving home
summaryEn: An HSK 5 reading titled “Things you only understand after leaving home”.
textZh:
讨论《离开家以后才懂的事》时，最容易犯的错误，是只看到表面的现象。
一个现象能够长期存在，通常不是因为某一个人突然做了决定。
它往往和市场、制度、技术、家庭选择以及社会心理同时有关。
因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
第一，是需求的变化。
年轻人、家庭和城市居民的生活方式正在改变。
他们更重视效率，也更重视体验。
如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
第二，是供给的变化。
企业为了获得用户，会不断降低门槛、改进流程、推出新的功能。
平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
但是，选择越多，人们有时候反而越焦虑。
第三，是社会评价的变化。
很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。
他们也会考虑这种选择是否显得体面、是否符合朋友的期待、是否适合放在社交媒体上。
这说明消费和身份之间的关系越来越紧密。
从这个角度看，离开家以后才懂的事不是孤立的事件。
它是现代城市生活的一面镜子。
当然，我们不能只把变化理解成进步。
新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。
比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。
信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。
服务更方便以后，个人数据和隐私也需要更多保护。
所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
这个问题没有一个简单答案。
但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
它会进入更深的社会理解。
进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
translation: When discussing 'Things you only understand after leaving home', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon. When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision. It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology. Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together. The first layer is changing demand. The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing. They care more about efficiency and also more about experience. If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept. The second layer is changing supply. To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions. Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster. However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become. The third layer is changing social evaluation. Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons. They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media. This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected. From this perspective, Things you only understand after leaving home is not an isolated event. It is a mirror of modern urban life. Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress. When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear. For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed. After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see. After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection. Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap. We must also ask to whom it transfers costs. Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for? This question has no simple answer. But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly. I believe a mature society should not reject new things. At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better. A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing. In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life. Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure. This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion. This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep. It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves. However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources. After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for. Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools. We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools. If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized. If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed. If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified. These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life. For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure. When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated. When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it. Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling. It will enter a deeper social understanding. Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time. In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life. Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。
sentenceExplanations.en: When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。
sentenceExplanations.en: For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。
sentenceExplanations.en: After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 服务更方便以后，个人数据和隐私也需要更多保护。
sentenceExplanations.en: After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
sentenceExplanations.en: Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
sentenceExplanations.en: We must also ask to whom it transfers costs.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We must also ask to whom it transfers costs.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
grammar.exampleEn: We must also ask to whom it transfers costs.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
sentenceExplanations.en: Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for?
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for?

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这个问题没有一个简单答案。
sentenceExplanations.en: This question has no simple answer.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This question has no simple answer.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
sentenceExplanations.en: But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
sentenceExplanations.en: I believe a mature society should not reject new things.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I believe a mature society should not reject new things.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
sentenceExplanations.en: At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
sentenceExplanations.en: A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
sentenceExplanations.en: In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life.

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
grammar.exampleEn: In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
sentenceExplanations.en: Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
sentenceExplanations.en: This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
sentenceExplanations.en: This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
sentenceExplanations.en: It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
sentenceExplanations.en: However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
sentenceExplanations.en: After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
sentenceExplanations.en: Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
sentenceExplanations.en: We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
sentenceExplanations.en: If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
grammar.exampleEn: If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
sentenceExplanations.en: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
grammar.exampleEn: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
sentenceExplanations.en: If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
grammar.exampleEn: If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
sentenceExplanations.en: These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
sentenceExplanations.en: For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
sentenceExplanations.en: When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
sentenceExplanations.en: When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
sentenceExplanations.en: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.

grammar.label: Necessary condition
grammar.explanationEn: The opening phrase sets a necessary condition, and the second marker introduces the result that can happen only under that condition.
grammar.exampleZh: 只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
grammar.exampleEn: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
grammar.exampleEn: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它会进入更深的社会理解。
sentenceExplanations.en: It will enter a deeper social understanding.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It will enter a deeper social understanding.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
sentenceExplanations.en: Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
sentenceExplanations.en: In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
sentenceExplanations.en: Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.

grammar.label: Gradual change
grammar.explanationEn: This form is followed by a quality or state to show gradual change over time: “more and more …”.
grammar.exampleZh: 现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
grammar.exampleEn: Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the main topic of this reading?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 离开家以后才懂的事
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: It mainly discusses things you only understand after leaving home.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The title and repeated details point to the central topic.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the writer's final thought?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The final sentence usually gives the conclusion or reflection.

titleZh: 狐狸和葡萄
summaryEn: An HSK 5 reading titled “The fox and the grapes”.
textZh:
讨论《狐狸和葡萄》时，最容易犯的错误，是只看到表面的现象。
一个现象能够长期存在，通常不是因为某一个人突然做了决定。
它往往和市场、制度、技术、家庭选择以及社会心理同时有关。
因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
第一，是需求的变化。
年轻人、家庭和城市居民的生活方式正在改变。
他们更重视效率，也更重视体验。
如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
第二，是供给的变化。
企业为了获得用户，会不断降低门槛、改进流程、推出新的功能。
平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
但是，选择越多，人们有时候反而越焦虑。
第三，是社会评价的变化。
很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。
他们也会考虑这种选择是否显得体面、是否符合朋友的期待、是否适合放在社交媒体上。
这说明消费和身份之间的关系越来越紧密。
从这个角度看，狐狸和葡萄不是孤立的事件。
它是现代城市生活的一面镜子。
当然，我们不能只把变化理解成进步。
新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。
比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。
信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。
服务更方便以后，个人数据和隐私也需要更多保护。
所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
这个问题没有一个简单答案。
但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
它会进入更深的社会理解。
进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
translation: When discussing 'The fox and the grapes', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon. When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision. It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology. Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together. The first layer is changing demand. The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing. They care more about efficiency and also more about experience. If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept. The second layer is changing supply. To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions. Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster. However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become. The third layer is changing social evaluation. Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons. They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media. This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected. From this perspective, The fox and the grapes is not an isolated event. It is a mirror of modern urban life. Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress. When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear. For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed. After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see. After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection. Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap. We must also ask to whom it transfers costs. Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for? This question has no simple answer. But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly. I believe a mature society should not reject new things. At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better. A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing. In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life. Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure. This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion. It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves. However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources. After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for. Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools. We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools. If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized. If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed. If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified. These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life. For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure. When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated. When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it. Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling. It will enter a deeper social understanding. Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time. In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life. Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency. This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep.

It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology. Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together. The first layer is changing demand. The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing. They care more about efficiency and also more about experience. If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept. The second layer is changing supply. To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions. Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster. However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become. The third layer is changing social evaluation. Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons. They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media. This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected. From this perspective, The fox and the grapes is not an isolated event. It is a mirror of modern urban life. Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress. When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear. For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed. After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see. After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection. Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap. We must also ask to whom it transfers costs. Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for? This question has no simple answer. But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly. I believe a mature society should not reject new things. At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better. A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing. In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life. Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure. This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion. It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves. However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources. After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for. Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools. We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools. If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized. If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed. If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified. These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life. For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure. When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated. When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it. Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling. It will enter a deeper social understanding. Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time. In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life. Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency. This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
paragraphTranslations.en: Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
paragraphTranslations.en: We must also ask to whom it transfers costs.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
paragraphTranslations.en: Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for?

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这个问题没有一个简单答案。
paragraphTranslations.en: This question has no simple answer.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
paragraphTranslations.en: But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
paragraphTranslations.en: I believe a mature society should not reject new things.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
paragraphTranslations.en: At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
paragraphTranslations.en: A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
paragraphTranslations.en: In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
paragraphTranslations.en: Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
paragraphTranslations.en: This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
paragraphTranslations.en: It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
paragraphTranslations.en: However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
paragraphTranslations.en: After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
paragraphTranslations.en: Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
paragraphTranslations.en: We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
paragraphTranslations.en: If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
paragraphTranslations.en: If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
paragraphTranslations.en: If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
paragraphTranslations.en: These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
paragraphTranslations.en: For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
paragraphTranslations.en: When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
paragraphTranslations.en: When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
paragraphTranslations.en: Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它会进入更深的社会理解。
paragraphTranslations.en: It will enter a deeper social understanding.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
paragraphTranslations.en: Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
paragraphTranslations.en: In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
paragraphTranslations.en: Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
paragraphTranslations.en: This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 讨论《狐狸和葡萄》时，最容易犯的错误，是只看到表面的现象。
sentenceExplanations.en: When discussing 'The fox and the grapes', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When discussing 'The fox and the grapes', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 一个现象能够长期存在，通常不是因为某一个人突然做了决定。
sentenceExplanations.en: When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它往往和市场、制度、技术、家庭选择以及社会心理同时有关。
sentenceExplanations.en: It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
sentenceExplanations.en: Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
grammar.exampleEn: Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第一，是需求的变化。
sentenceExplanations.en: The first layer is changing demand.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The first layer is changing demand.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 年轻人、家庭和城市居民的生活方式正在改变。
sentenceExplanations.en: The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 他们更重视效率，也更重视体验。
sentenceExplanations.en: They care more about efficiency and also more about experience.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: They care more about efficiency and also more about experience.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
sentenceExplanations.en: If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
grammar.exampleEn: If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第二，是供给的变化。
sentenceExplanations.en: The second layer is changing supply.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The second layer is changing supply.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 企业为了获得用户，会不断降低门槛、改进流程、推出新的功能。
sentenceExplanations.en: To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
sentenceExplanations.en: Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
grammar.exampleEn: Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 但是，选择越多，人们有时候反而越焦虑。
sentenceExplanations.en: However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第三，是社会评价的变化。
sentenceExplanations.en: The third layer is changing social evaluation.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The third layer is changing social evaluation.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。
sentenceExplanations.en: Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons.

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。
grammar.exampleEn: Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 他们也会考虑这种选择是否显得体面、是否符合朋友的期待、是否适合放在社交媒体上。
sentenceExplanations.en: They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这说明消费和身份之间的关系越来越紧密。
sentenceExplanations.en: This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected.

grammar.label: Gradual change
grammar.explanationEn: This form is followed by a quality or state to show gradual change over time: “more and more …”.

grammar.exampleEn: This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 从这个角度看，狐狸和葡萄不是孤立的事件。

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它是现代城市生活的一面镜子。
sentenceExplanations.en: It is a mirror of modern urban life.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It is a mirror of modern urban life.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 当然，我们不能只把变化理解成进步。
sentenceExplanations.en: Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。

sentenceExplanations.zh: 比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。

sentenceExplanations.zh: 信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。

sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see.

titleZh: 第一次自己租房
summaryEn: An HSK 5 reading titled “Renting my first apartment”.
textZh:
讨论《第一次自己租房》时，最容易犯的错误，是只看到表面的现象。
一个现象能够长期存在，通常不是因为某一个人突然做了决定。
它往往和市场、制度、技术、家庭选择以及社会心理同时有关。
因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
第一，是需求的变化。
年轻人、家庭和城市居民的生活方式正在改变。
他们更重视效率，也更重视体验。
如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
第二，是供给的变化。
企业为了获得用户，会不断降低门槛、改进流程、推出新的功能。
平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
但是，选择越多，人们有时候反而越焦虑。
第三，是社会评价的变化。
很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。
他们也会考虑这种选择是否显得体面、是否符合朋友的期待、是否适合放在社交媒体上。
这说明消费和身份之间的关系越来越紧密。
从这个角度看，第一次自己租房不是孤立的事件。
它是现代城市生活的一面镜子。
当然，我们不能只把变化理解成进步。
新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。
比如，效率提高以后，人的休息时间可能被继续压缩。
信息变多以后，真正重要的信息反而不容易被看见。
服务更方便以后，个人数据和隐私也需要更多保护。
所以，评价一个变化，不能只问它快不快、便宜不便宜。
还要问它把成本转移给了谁。
它让哪些人受益，又让哪些人更难参与。
这个问题没有一个简单答案。
但是提出问题本身，就能帮助我们看得更清楚。
我认为，一个成熟的社会不应该拒绝新事物。
同时，它也不应该盲目相信新事物一定更好。
更合理的态度，是在使用中观察，在观察中调整。
这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
否则，所谓的便利也可能变成另一种压力。
这也是这类话题值得被认真阅读和讨论的原因。
可是，人的注意力和身体并不是无限的资源。
当所有流程都被压缩以后，休息反而成了一种需要主动争取的东西。
因此，讨论现代生活不能只讨论工具。
还要讨论工具背后的节奏。
一个系统如果只奖励速度，慢的人就会不断被边缘化。
一个市场如果只追求增长，长期信任就可能被消耗。
一个平台如果只看数据，人的复杂经验就会被简化。
这些问题听起来抽象，但都能在日常生活中看到。
例如，我们点外卖时希望更快，却很少想到配送员的时间压力。
我们看短视频时希望推荐更准，却很少想到自己的注意力被怎样分配。
我们享受便利时，也需要看见便利背后的劳动和规则。
只有这样，讨论才不会停在个人感受上。
它会进入更深的社会理解。
进一步说，这类现象还会改变人们对时间的理解。
过去，等待常常被看成生活的一部分。
现在，等待越来越容易被理解成效率低下。
这种观念的改变看似自然，其实影响很深。
它让服务行业不断加速，也让个人不断要求自己更快。
translation: When discussing 'Renting my first apartment', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon. When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision. It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology. Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together. The first layer is changing demand. The lifestyles of young people, families, and urban residents are changing. They care more about efficiency and also more about experience. If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept. The second layer is changing supply. To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions. Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster. However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become. The third layer is changing social evaluation. Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons. They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media. This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected. From this perspective, Renting my first apartment is not an isolated event. It is a mirror of modern urban life. Of course, we cannot understand every change simply as progress. When new opportunities appear, new problems also appear. For example, after efficiency improves, people's rest time may continue to be compressed. After information increases, truly important information may become harder to see. After services become more convenient, personal data and privacy also need more protection. Therefore, when evaluating a change, we cannot only ask whether it is fast or cheap. We must also ask to whom it transfers costs. Whom does it benefit, and whom does it make participation harder for? This question has no simple answer. But raising the question itself helps us see more clearly. I believe a mature society should not reject new things. At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better. A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing. In this way, technology and markets can truly serve human life. Otherwise, so-called convenience may become another kind of pressure. This is why topics like this deserve serious reading and discussion. However, human attention and the body are not unlimited resources. After all processes are compressed, rest becomes something one must actively fight for. Therefore, discussing modern life cannot be only about tools. We must also discuss the rhythm behind the tools. If a system rewards only speed, slower people will continually be marginalized. If a market pursues only growth, long-term trust may be consumed. If a platform looks only at data, complex human experience will be simplified. These issues sound abstract, but they can all be seen in everyday life. For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure. When watching short videos, we want recommendations to be more accurate but rarely think about how our attention is allocated. When enjoying convenience, we also need to see the labor and rules behind it. Only in this way will discussion not stop at personal feeling. It will enter a deeper social understanding. Going further, this kind of phenomenon also changes how people understand time. In the past, waiting was often seen as part of life. Now, waiting is increasingly understood as inefficiency. This change in perception seems natural, but its influence is deep. It pushes service industries to speed up and makes individuals demand more speed from themselves.

If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept. The second layer is changing supply. At the same time, it should not blindly believe that new things are necessarily better. A more reasonable attitude is to observe while using, and adjust while observing. For example, when ordering delivery, we want it faster but rarely think about the courier's time pressure. paragraphTranslations.zh: 讨论《第一次自己租房》时，最容易犯的错误，是只看到表面的现象。
paragraphTranslations.en: When discussing 'Renting my first apartment', the easiest mistake is to see only the surface phenomenon.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 一个现象能够长期存在，通常不是因为某一个人突然做了决定。
paragraphTranslations.en: When a phenomenon persists for a long time, it is usually not because one person suddenly made a decision.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它往往和市场、制度、技术、家庭选择以及社会心理同时有关。
paragraphTranslations.en: It is often connected at the same time to markets, institutions, technology, family choices, and social psychology.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 因此，理解它需要把几个层面放在一起看。
paragraphTranslations.en: Therefore, understanding it requires looking at several layers together.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 第一，是需求的变化。
paragraphTranslations.en: The first layer is changing demand.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 他们更重视效率，也更重视体验。

paragraphTranslations.zh: 如果一种服务既能节省时间，又能带来比较好的感受，它就容易被接受。
paragraphTranslations.en: If a service can both save time and provide a good experience, it is easy to accept.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 第二，是供给的变化。
paragraphTranslations.en: The second layer is changing supply.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 企业为了获得用户，会不断降低门槛、改进流程、推出新的功能。
paragraphTranslations.en: To gain users, companies keep lowering barriers, improving processes, and launching new functions.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 平台把原来分散的信息集中起来，让选择变得更快。
paragraphTranslations.en: Platforms gather formerly scattered information and make choices faster.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 但是，选择越多，人们有时候反而越焦虑。
paragraphTranslations.en: However, the more choices there are, the more anxious people sometimes become.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 第三，是社会评价的变化。
paragraphTranslations.en: The third layer is changing social evaluation.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 很多人并不是单纯为了实用才做选择。
paragraphTranslations.en: Many people do not make choices purely for practical reasons.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 他们也会考虑这种选择是否显得体面、是否符合朋友的期待、是否适合放在社交媒体上。
paragraphTranslations.en: They also consider whether the choice looks respectable, whether it fits friends' expectations, and whether it is suitable for social media.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这说明消费和身份之间的关系越来越紧密。
paragraphTranslations.en: This shows that consumption and identity are becoming increasingly connected.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 从这个角度看，第一次自己租房不是孤立的事件。
paragraphTranslations.en: From this perspective, Renting my first apartment is not an isolated event.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它是现代城市生活的一面镜子。
paragraphTranslations.en: It is a mirror of modern urban life.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 当然，我们不能只把变化理解成进步。

paragraphTranslations.zh: 新机会出现的同时，新问题也会出现。

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这样，技术和市场才能真正服务于人的生活。
